text:
There was a farmer named Oliver who lived deep in the woods. Every morning, as dawn crept across the dew-covered leaves, Oliver would go for his walk. On his walks, he was often surprised by visiting animals. Once, as a bird fluttered down beside him, he laughed.

"That's quite the plumage you've got there," Oliver said warmly.

The bird squawked and fluttered its wings. "Well actually, that is a beautiful shade of purple."

Oliver stopped to admire it. "You've got so much personality."

Their conversation continued until a dog approached. The bird chirped loudly. Oliver gently put it back into the forest. Later, he found a shiny pebble on the forest floor.

"What cleverness," he said. "But it doesn't look very useful."

He thought about it. "Wait, maybe. Maybe they're tiny pebble knights!"

He imagined tiny birds swooping around. He laughed heartily. "I'd fight those
